mappings.ccDependency: `claude` launches a usable interactive session on demand in any cwd; optimistic .working assumes SessionStart/Stop will settle it.
mappings.opencodeMechanism: Bare `opencode` launches on demand in any cwd; `session.created` + `session.idle` bus events replace SessionStart/Stop for the optimistic-working settle.
mappings.approach: newClaude/spawnClaude become newOpencode/spawnOpencode constructing Session(kind:.opencode); every surface (palette, nav menu, ControlServer automation, CommentMode, feedback-fix) funnels unchanged. Only the launch command word and the status-settling event source change. Purely mechanical rename + event rewire once touchpoints 1/5/8 land.

mappings.ccDependency: `claude --settings <inline-json>` accepts a per-launch hooks map keyed by exact CC event names, each a type:command hook; a PATH-shim deep-merges Synth's hooks into the user's settings before exec.
mappings.opencodeMechanism: Two native, shim-free channels: (a) a global plugin at `~/.config/opencode/plugin/` (auto-loaded, subscribes to the full event set — tool.execute.before/after, session.*, message.*, permission.asked/replied, etc.); (b) `opencode serve` + `@opencode-ai/sdk` `event.subscribe()` / GET `/event` SSE stream. Config comes from opencode.json (project/global) or `OPENCODE_CONFIG`.
mappings.approach: Biggest architectural win of the port: delete the PATH-shim entirely. Instead install one global Synth plugin (or run a persistent `opencode serve` that Synth already owns) and consume the real event bus. This removes the deep-merge-into-user-settings dance, the per-launch inline-JSON injection, and the whole synth-hook launch role. There's no inline `--settings` flag, but you don't need per-launch injection because a single global plugin observes every session, and any per-worktree config goes in a written `.opencode/opencode.json` / `OPENCODE_CONFIG`. Map CC event names → opencode events: SessionStart→session.created, Stop/idle→session.idle, PermissionRequest→permission.asked, PostToolUse→tool.execute.after, UserPromptSubmit→message.updated.
mappings.gap: No per-invocation inline settings object; hooks are a global/project plugin (or server consumer), not injected per `exec`. Event names and payload shapes differ from CC's, so buildSettingsJSON and Hooks.swift's runEvent classifier must be rewritten as a plugin/SDK consumer. Net simpler, but not a code-level 1:1.

mappings.ccDependency: `claude --session-id <uuid>` sets a Synth-minted conversation id (rejected alongside --resume/--continue); session_id echoes on every hook payload for capture.
mappings.opencodeMechanism: opencode mints its own session id — no caller-supplied-id flag. `POST /session` returns a Session `{ id, title }`; the TUI attaches to an existing id via `--session <id>`; `session.created` events carry the id.
mappings.approach: Invert the flow: instead of minting a UUID and passing it in, either (a) capture opencode's own id from the `session.created` event / `session.list` and store it as Session.opencodeSessionID, or (b) create the session first via server `POST /session` (get its id), then boot the TUI attached to it with `exec opencode --session <id>`. Path (b) preserves Synth's 'know the id before/at launch' invariant cleanly.
mappings.gap: No `--session-id` create flag; you cannot force a Synth-chosen UUID onto the TUI. Requires either post-hoc id capture from events or a create-then-attach two-step via the server.

mappings.ccDependency: `claude --resume <session-id>` restores a prior conversation; hooks still fire under resume; failed resume drops to a bare shell.
mappings.opencodeMechanism: `opencode --session <id>` / `-s` resumes a specific session, `--continue`/`-c` resumes the last; docs state -c/-s work across 'run, TUI, attach'. Events still fire under resume since the plugin/server is global.
mappings.approach: GhosttySurfaceView emits `exec opencode --session '<id>'<flags>` (shell-quoted) from the stored opencodeSessionID instead of `--resume`. Status wiring keeps working because the global plugin/server observes the resumed session regardless. Same failed-resume fallback semantics apply (drop to bare shell, gate comment delivery on liveness).
mappings.gap: Flag spelling differs (`--session <id>` vs `--resume <id>`); confirm the bare-TUI form of `-s` behaves as documented across TUI (docs say yes).

mappings.touchpoint: Inherited CLAUDE_CODE_* markers scrubbed before spawn
mappings.ccDependency: CC leaves env markers (esp. CLAUDE_CODE_CHILD_SESSION) that make a claude spawned inside a claude behave as a subagent with no on-disk transcript; Synth scrubs them so spawns are top-level/resumable.
mappings.opencodeMechanism: opencode does not spawn subagents by exec-nesting — the task tool creates child sessions internally within the same server process. A separately-exec'd `opencode` is always a fresh top-level process; no unresumable-subagent env marker exists.
mappings.approach: Largely moot: the problem this touchpoint solves doesn't arise, because opencode's subagent model is in-process (task tool → child session), not process-nesting. A Synth-launched `opencode` inside an opencode-driven shell just starts its own top-level session. Recommend a light audit of opencode's env (OPENCODE_* vars, e.g. OPENCODE_CLIENT) and strip any that would confuse a nested launch, but no CHILD_SESSION-style scrub is required.
mappings.gap: None functionally; residual low-confidence on whether any OPENCODE_* env var (e.g. an internal server/session pointer) leaks into nested spawns and needs a defensive unset — undocumented, so verify empirically.

mappings.ccDependency: CC honors settings key `preferredNotifChannel: notifications_disabled` to suppress its built-in OSC/terminal notification channel.
mappings.opencodeMechanism: No documented equivalent settings key. opencode surfaces notifications via the bus (`tui.toast.show`) and there's no confirmed OSC/OS-notification channel that would double up with Synth's deck.
mappings.approach: Likely a non-issue: opencode's TUI notifications are in-app toasts, not the OS/terminal channel CC uses, so the double-notification problem may not exist. If a suppressible channel does emerge, handle it via config or by not surfacing opencode toasts. Treat as verify-then-maybe-noop rather than a required setting.
mappings.gap: No `preferredNotifChannel` analog documented; unknown whether opencode emits an OS/terminal notification that needs silencing. If it does, no config key is documented to disable just that channel.

mappings.touchpoint: spawned-claude command excluded from per-command term reporting
mappings.ccDependency: Synth's zsh preexec strips a leading `exec ` and returns early when the command word is `claude`, so CC's hook-driven status owns the row instead of the coarse shell dot.
mappings.opencodeMechanism: Same shell-level mechanism; only the matched command word changes. opencode's own status comes from the plugin/event bus, exactly the role CC's hooks played.
mappings.approach: Change the preexec early-return to match command word `opencode` (after stripping leading `exec `) instead of `claude`. The rationale is unchanged: defer the row's status to opencode's fine-grained event-driven signal so the coarse per-command dot doesn't double-track it.
